response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for this role: SoC/IP modeling, hardware performance architecture, AI accelerator or GPU workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. His low-latency DLang microservices work at Dunnhumby demonstrates software performance sensibility, but at the application level rather than the silicon/system-architecture level this principal role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Some exposure to embedded systems, hardware projects (robotic arms), and C/C++ tooling at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No deep learning, AI accelerator, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from a principal-level hardware performance architect profile

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU expertise
Strong general engineering seniority and low-latency systems work
